rendering_strategy.rationale: This platform has three distinct content tiers requiring different rendering strategies: (1) Static editorial content (onsen guides, etiquette articles, regional spotlights) should use SSG for maximum performance and SEO — these pages change infrequently and benefit from CDN edge caching. (2) Ryokan listing pages should use ISR with 60-second revalidation — property details change infrequently but availability/pricing may update, and ISR provides SEO benefits of static generation with near-real-time data freshness. (3) Search results, booking flow, user dashboard, and community features require SSR for personalized, real-time content. Next.js App Router supports all three strategies per-route, making this hybrid approach natural without additional infrastructure.

alternatives_considered.reason_rejected: Unnecessary server load for static editorial content and ryokan detail pages that change infrequently. Would increase Vercel serverless function invocations and cost without UX benefit.
Incompatible with real-time search, availability checking, and personalized booking flows. Build times would be excessive with thousands of listing pages and multilingual content.

Poor SEO for listing and editorial pages — critical for organic discovery by international travelers searching 'best onsen ryokan in Hakone'. Also poor initial load performance on mobile networks in rural Japan.

packages.name: next
packages.purpose: Core framework — App Router for hybrid rendering, Server Components for streaming, Route Handlers for API endpoints, built-in image optimization for property photos

packages.name: next-intl
packages.purpose: Internationalization — bilingual Japanese/English support with message catalogs, locale-aware routing (/ja and /en prefixes), date/currency formatting per locale

packages.name: next-auth
packages.purpose: Authentication — OAuth providers (Google, LINE for Japanese users), email magic links for international users. NextAuth v5 beta aligns with App Router patterns

packages.name: @supabase/supabase-js
packages.purpose: Database client — Postgres via Supabase for ryokan listings, bookings, reviews, user profiles. Includes real-time subscriptions for availability updates and Row Level Security for multi-tenant owner dashboards

packages.name: stripe
packages.purpose: Payment processing — multi-currency support (JPY primary), Connect for marketplace payouts to ryokan owners, escrow-style payment holds, webhook handling for booking confirmations

packages.name: zod
packages.purpose: Schema validation — type-safe validation for booking forms, search parameters, API request/response shapes, and environment variable validation

packages.name: react-hook-form
packages.purpose: Form management — performant form handling for multi-step booking flow, search filters, review submission, and ryokan owner dashboard forms

packages.name: @tanstack/react-query
packages.purpose: Server state management — caching and synchronization for availability checks, search results pagination, optimistic updates on wishlist/favorites

packages.name: date-fns
packages.purpose: Date utilities — check-in/check-out date manipulation, Japanese calendar support, relative date formatting for reviews, availability calendar logic

packages.name: react-map-gl
packages.purpose: Map integration — interactive Mapbox GL map for onsen region discovery, ryokan location pins with clustering, responsive map with touch gestures for mobile

packages.name: embla-carousel-react
packages.purpose: Image carousel — lightweight, touch-friendly carousel for ryokan photo galleries, seasonal photo slideshows, and featured collections

packages.name: nuqs
packages.purpose: URL search params management — type-safe URL state for search filters (region, price range, onsen type, bath type) enabling shareable/bookmarkable search URLs

external_apis.name: Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Storage)
external_apis.purpose: Primary database for ryokan listings, bookings, reviews, user data. Storage for property images. Auth as fallback/supplement to NextAuth.
external_apis.rate_limits: Free tier: 500MB database, 1GB storage, 50K monthly active users. Pro tier ($25/mo): 8GB database, 100GB storage, unlimited MAU. No API rate limits on Pro tier.
external_apis.cost_implications: Pro tier sufficient for MVP. At scale (10K+ bookings/month), expect $100-300/month for database + storage. Egress costs for image serving mitigated by Vercel CDN caching.
external_apis.fallback: Neon Postgres as database alternative; Cloudflare R2 for image storage if Supabase costs escalate

external_apis.name: Stripe Payments + Connect
external_apis.purpose: Guest payments, multi-currency conversion (JPY ↔ USD/EUR/etc.), marketplace payouts to ryokan owners via Stripe Connect, escrow holds
external_apis.rate_limits: 100 read requests/second, 25 write requests/second per API key. No practical limit for booking volumes at MVP/growth stage.
external_apis.cost_implications: 2.9% + $0.30 per domestic transaction, +1% for international cards, +1% for currency conversion. Stripe Connect: additional 0.25% + $0.25 per payout. At $1M GMV/year, payment processing costs ~$35K-$45K.
external_apis.fallback: No viable alternative with equivalent multi-currency + marketplace payout support. PAY.JP could supplement for Japan-domestic transactions.

external_apis.name: Mapbox GL
external_apis.purpose: Interactive map for onsen region discovery, geocoding for ryokan locations, directions/transit info to ryokan from nearest station
external_apis.rate_limits: Free tier: 50K map loads/month, 100K geocoding requests/month. Beyond free tier: $5 per 1,000 map loads.
external_apis.cost_implications: Free tier sufficient for first 6 months. At 100K monthly visitors with 50% using map: ~$250/month. Japan map tiles well-supported with Japanese labels.
external_apis.fallback: Google Maps Platform (more expensive at $7/1000 loads but better Japan POI data) or OpenStreetMap/MapLibre (free but requires self-hosting tiles)

external_apis.name: Vercel Image Optimization
external_apis.purpose: On-the-fly image resizing, WebP/AVIF conversion, and CDN delivery for ryokan property photos — critical for mobile performance and visual storytelling
external_apis.rate_limits: Pro plan: 5,000 source images. Enterprise: unlimited. Optimized images served from edge CDN with no per-request limit.
external_apis.cost_implications: Included in Vercel Pro plan ($20/month per team member). At scale with 2,000+ ryokan listings × 10-20 photos each, may need Enterprise plan or external image CDN.
external_apis.fallback: Cloudinary (free tier: 25K transformations/month, $89/month Plus plan) or imgix for dedicated image optimization

external_apis.name: SendGrid / Resend
external_apis.purpose: Booking confirmation emails, cancellation notifications, review request emails, owner dashboard alerts (bilingual JP/EN templates)
external_apis.rate_limits: Resend free tier: 3,000 emails/month, 100 emails/day. Pro: $20/month for 50K emails/month.
external_apis.cost_implications: Free tier for MVP, Pro tier ($20/month) sufficient through first year of operation.
external_apis.fallback: SendGrid free tier (100 emails/day) or Supabase Edge Functions with SMTP relay

serverless_function_timeout.impact: Booking flow involving Stripe payment intent creation + Supabase transaction should complete within 10-15 seconds. Availability search queries across multiple ryokan may approach 5-10 seconds for complex filters. Pro plan (60s) provides sufficient headroom.
serverless_function_timeout.mitigation: Use streaming SSR for search results (show results progressively). Implement Edge Runtime for lightweight API routes (auth checks, simple queries). Move heavy operations (image processing, email sending) to background jobs via Vercel Cron or Inngest.

bundle_size.impact: react-map-gl + Mapbox GL JS adds ~200KB to client bundle. Stripe.js loaded async. next-intl message catalogs for JP+EN may add 50-100KB. Total client bundle estimated at 400-600KB (compressed), well within limits.
bundle_size.mitigation: Lazy-load map component (dynamic import with ssr: false). Code-split by route. Use next/dynamic for heavy components like image carousels and date pickers.

cold_start.impact: First request after idle period may show 500ms-1s delay on SSR pages (search, booking). Not critical for this use case — users expect slight load time on travel booking sites.
cold_start.mitigation: Use Edge Runtime for latency-sensitive routes (auth middleware, locale detection). Keep serverless functions lean. ISR for listing pages eliminates cold starts for most page views.

deployment_regions.default: Vercel automatically deploys to global edge network. Serverless functions default to iad1 (US East).
deployment_regions.recommendation: Set serverless function region to hnd1 (Tokyo) for lowest latency to Supabase (hosted in Tokyo region) and Japanese ryokan owner dashboard users. Edge functions run globally for international traveler UX.

browser_apis.purpose: Enable 'nearby onsen' search for travelers currently in Japan, auto-center map on user location
browser_apis.fallback: Default map view centered on Japan with popular onsen regions highlighted. IP-based geolocation as secondary signal. Manual region selection always available.

browser_apis.purpose: Lazy-load ryokan listing images as user scrolls through search results, infinite scroll pagination for reviews
browser_apis.fallback: Supported in all modern browsers (97%+ global coverage). No polyfill needed — degrade to eager loading on unsupported browsers.

browser_apis.purpose: Native sharing of ryokan listings and onsen collections on mobile devices (share to LINE, WhatsApp, etc.)
browser_apis.fallback: Custom share modal with copy-to-clipboard link + social media share buttons (LINE, Twitter, Facebook). Web Share API enhances but is not required.

browser_apis.purpose: Offline caching of previously viewed ryokan listings for travelers in rural areas with poor connectivity (many onsen regions have limited mobile data)
browser_apis.fallback: Standard browser caching via Cache-Control headers. PWA manifest for add-to-homescreen. Offline page with saved favorites. next-pwa package can automate Service Worker generation.

browser_apis.purpose: Locale-aware formatting of prices (¥25,000 vs $167), dates (2026年3月22日 vs March 22, 2026), and relative time in reviews
browser_apis.fallback: Fully supported in all modern browsers. date-fns locale modules provide additional formatting consistency.

key_strengths: Next.js App Router perfectly supports the hybrid rendering needs (SSG editorial + ISR listings + SSR search/booking)
Supabase provides integrated database + auth + storage without managing infrastructure
Stripe Connect is purpose-built for marketplace payment flows with multi-currency support
Vercel Tokyo region (hnd1) ensures low latency for the primary market
All npm packages are actively maintained with recent releases (verified March 2026)
key_risks: Image-heavy platform may push Vercel image optimization limits on Pro plan — monitor and plan for Cloudinary fallback
Mapbox GL JS client bundle size requires careful lazy-loading to maintain mobile performance
Bilingual content doubles the content management surface area — consider CMS integration (Sanity/Contentful) if editorial volume grows
feasibility_summary.estimated_mvp_timeline: 10-14 weeks for core booking flow (search → listing → booking → confirmation) with 2-language support

estimated_monthly_infrastructure_cost.mvp_phase: $65-$100 (Vercel Pro $20 + Supabase Pro $25 + domain + Mapbox free tier + Resend free tier)
estimated_monthly_infrastructure_cost.growth_phase_10k_bookings_per_month: $500-$1,200 (Vercel Pro + Supabase scale + Mapbox + image optimization + email)
